title: Dara's Bouncy Ball Experiment
synopsis: Dara loves bouncing balls and wants to find out which ball bounces the highest. With help from her friends Marcus and Lily, she conducts a fun science experiment that leads to surprising and silly results.

pages.text: Dara loved balls. She had red balls, blue balls, and yellow balls. She had big balls and little balls. But most of all, Dara loved bouncy balls! Every morning, she would bounce her favorite purple ball in the backyard. Bounce, bounce, bounce!

illustration.description: A cheerful girl with dark curly hair and warm brown skin bouncing a bright purple ball in a sunny backyard. She's wearing a colorful striped shirt and has a huge smile. Green grass and blue sky with puffy white clouds. The ball is captured mid-bounce with motion lines.
illustration.alt: Dara bouncing her purple ball in the backyard

pages.text: One day, Dara looked at all her balls. "I wonder which ball bounces the highest?" she said. She picked up her tennis ball. Then she picked up her rubber ball. "How can I find out?" Dara scratched her head and thought hard.

illustration.description: Dara sitting cross-legged on the floor surrounded by colorful balls of different sizes and types. She has one finger to her chin in a thinking pose, with question marks floating above her head. The balls are arranged in a rainbow of colors around her.
illustration.alt: Dara thinking while surrounded by different balls

pages.text: "I know!" Dara jumped up. "I can do an experiment!" She had learned about experiments in school. Her teacher, Mrs. Chen, said experiments help you find answers. Dara was excited. This would be the best experiment ever!

illustration.description: Dara leaping up with both arms raised in excitement, her eyes sparkling with joy. A lightbulb appears above her head glowing bright yellow. The room is filled with warm light and her expression shows pure enthusiasm and determination.
illustration.alt: Dara having a eureka moment about doing an experiment

pages.text: Dara called her friend Marcus. "Want to help me with a ball experiment?" she asked. "Yes!" said Marcus. "I love experiments!" Next, Dara called her friend Lily. "Can you come over?" Dara asked. "We're doing science!" "Cool!" said Lily. "I'll bring my bouncy ball too!"

illustration.description: Dara talking on a bright yellow phone with a big smile. Through the window, we can see Marcus (a boy with red hair and freckles) and Lily (a girl with black hair in pigtails and East Asian features) walking up to her house, both carrying colorful balls and looking excited.
illustration.alt: Dara on the phone while her friends approach her house

pages.text: Soon Marcus and Lily arrived. "Look at all these balls!" said Marcus. His eyes were wide. Lily giggled. "This looks like a ball store!" They had tennis balls, rubber balls, ping pong balls, and bouncy balls. "Which one will win?" asked Lily.

illustration.description: Three children standing in a room with balls scattered everywhere. Marcus (pale skin, red curly hair, green t-shirt) has his mouth open in amazement. Lily (wearing a pink dress, black pigtails) is giggling with her hands over her mouth. Dara stands proudly in the middle with her arms spread wide.
illustration.alt: The three friends amazed by all the different balls

pages.text: "Let's find out!" said Dara. "But first, we need a plan." Marcus nodded. "My dad says good experiments need plans." Lily clapped her hands. "This is going to be so much fun!" The three friends sat in a circle to make their plan.

illustration.description: The three children sitting in a circle on a colorful rug, leaning in toward each other. Dara is holding a notepad and pencil. They all look focused and excited, with various balls arranged around them in the background. Warm afternoon sunlight streams through a nearby window.
illustration.alt: The friends planning their experiment together

pages.text: The friends went outside. "We need to drop each ball from the same height," said Dara. Marcus found a chair. "Good thinking!" said Lily. Dara climbed on the chair. She held the tennis ball high. "Ready?" she called. "Ready!" her friends shouted.

illustration.description: Dara standing on a wooden chair in the backyard, holding a yellow tennis ball high above her head. Marcus and Lily stand nearby watching intently. The setting is a bright sunny day with green grass and a wooden fence in the background. Everyone looks focused and excited.
illustration.alt: Dara preparing to drop the tennis ball from the chair

pages.text: Dara dropped the tennis ball. Bounce! It went up to her knee. "Tennis ball - knee high!" called Marcus. He was keeping track. Next came the rubber ball. Bounce! It went up to Dara's waist. "Rubber ball - waist high!" said Lily. She was getting excited.

illustration.description: Action scene showing the tennis ball and rubber ball bouncing at different heights next to Dara. Motion lines show the bouncing paths. Marcus is pointing and calling out measurements while Lily watches with wide eyes. The balls are captured at their highest bounce points with dotted lines showing their trajectories.
illustration.alt: The balls bouncing to different heights during the experiment

pages.text: Then Dara tried the ping pong ball. It barely bounced at all! "That's funny!" laughed Marcus. The ping pong ball just rolled away. Finally, Dara held up her purple bouncy ball. "This is my favorite," she said. "Let's see what happens!"

illustration.description: Dara holding her bright purple bouncy ball with anticipation while the white ping pong ball sits sadly on the ground nearby, barely having bounced. Marcus is laughing and pointing at the ping pong ball. Lily is covering her mouth, trying not to giggle. The scene shows the contrast between the exciting bouncy ball and the disappointing ping pong ball.
illustration.alt: Dara with her bouncy ball while the ping pong ball fails to bounce

pages.text: Dara dropped her purple bouncy ball. BOUNCE! It went way up high! Higher than her head! Higher than the chair! "Wow!" shouted all three friends. But the ball didn't stop bouncing. It bounced and bounced and bounced!

illustration.description: The purple bouncy ball soaring high above everyone's heads with exaggerated motion lines. All three children are looking up with their mouths wide open in amazement, their arms raised. The ball appears to be bouncing much higher than expected, almost reaching tree level. Bright blue sky background with the ball creating a dramatic arc.
illustration.alt: The bouncy ball flying surprisingly high while the friends watch in amazement

pages.text: The bouncy ball bounced into the garden. It bounced off a flower. It bounced off the fence. It bounced off Dad's hat! "Sorry, Dad!" called Dara. Dad laughed. "That's some experiment!" he said. The ball kept bouncing around the yard.

illustration.description: A chaotic but fun scene showing the purple ball bouncing off various objects in the backyard. Dad (a tall man with a beard and baseball cap askew) is laughing good-naturedly. The ball's path is shown with dotted lines bouncing off flowers, the fence, and Dad's hat. The three kids are running around trying to follow the ball's crazy path.
illustration.alt: The ball bouncing wildly around the yard, hitting Dad's hat

pages.text: Finally, the ball stopped bouncing. The friends ran over to get it. "The bouncy ball wins!" said Dara. "That was the best experiment ever!" said Marcus. Lily nodded. "Can we do it again?" All three friends looked at each other and smiled. "YES!" they shouted together.

illustration.description: The three friends standing together in a group hug, all smiling widely with the purple bouncy ball in the center of their circle. The backyard looks slightly messy from their experiment but everyone looks happy and satisfied. Golden afternoon light gives the scene a warm, celebratory feeling. Dad can be seen in the background, still chuckling.
illustration.alt: The friends celebrating their successful experiment together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What does Dara love to do with balls?
questions.explanation: Dara loves bouncing balls! The story says she loved bouncy balls most of all.

questions.question: How many friends helped Dara with her experiment?
questions.explanation: Marcus and Lily helped Dara, so two friends helped her with the experiment.

questions.question: Which ball bounced the highest?
questions.explanation: The purple bouncy ball bounced the highest - even higher than Dara's head!

questions.question: What did the bouncy ball hit in the yard?
questions.explanation: The bouncy ball bounced off Dad's hat, and Dara said 'Sorry, Dad!'

questions.question: How did the friends feel about doing the experiment again?
questions.explanation: When Lily asked if they could do it again, all three friends shouted 'YES!' together, showing they were excited.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from Dara's experiment.

blanks.sentence: Dara loved to _____ balls in her backyard.
She wanted to find out which ball bounced the _____.
Marcus and _____ came to help with the experiment.

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Put the experiment steps in the right order by choosing what happened next.

blanks.sentence: The tennis ball bounced up to her _____.
The purple ball bounced off Dad's _____!
